summary: How five-year-old "talent" Adam Azim became IBO light-welterweight champion.

title: 'It's been written' - Arsenal players look ahead to Champions League final
summary: Before the Champions League final against Paris Saint-Germain on Saturday, Arsenal players Leandro Trossard, Myles Lewis-Skelly, Declan Rice and Eberechi Eze talk about what winning the trophy would mean to them.

title: AI costs begin to bite as agents may increase token demand by 24 times, says Goldman Sachs report — Uber and Microsoft among companies feeling the bite of tokenized billing
summary: Major tech companies are considering refining their approaches to AI, as rising token costs and increased token demand from AI agents make the costs harder to justify, with limited return on the investment.

title: Valve hikes Steam Deck OLED prices — 512GB is now $789, while 1TB climbs to $949
summary: The Valve Steam Deck is seeing price hikes as high as $300 as the component shortage continues.

title: Why Putellas to London City would be biggest signing in WSL history
summary: Barcelona icon Alexia Putellas is moving on this summer, so BBC Sport investigates the impact of a potential switch to London City Lionesses.

title: Italian council sets 200% tax on data center development in agricultural zones — aims to spur the use of old industrial areas instead and limit environmental impact
summary: This 200% tax will make it more expensive to build in rural areas and is aimed at spurring data center development in disused industrial zones instead.

title: Get your hands on a 2TB Samsung 990 Pro SSD for under $390 — $250 savings brings one of the fastest PCIe 4.0 SSDs to its lowest price in months
summary: Samsung's 990 Pro 2TB is one of the fastest PCIe 4.0 M.2 SSDs around, delivering excellent performance and efficiency and backed by a 5-year warranty - at $389.99, it's not the cheapest, but is a well-rounded storage upgrade for any build.

title: Nvidia offers restricted access to Vera CPU in first round of Linux benchmarks - 88-core monster competes with or beats Epyc and Xeon in selected tests
summary: NVIDIA's new server CPU doesn't win outright in most tests, but it's running very close to AMD's EPYC, which is incredible for a first-generation custom server core from NVIDIA.

title: Erin Brockovich starts tracking AI data centers, calls on affected communities to submit issues — website shows more than 2,700 reports from across the US raising various concerns

title: China adds homegrown AI chips to 'secure and reliable' procurement list for the first time — nine options added as move away from Nvidia continues
summary: The certifications are valid for three years and were issued jointly by the China Information Technology Security Evaluation Centre and the National Secrecy Science and Technology Evaluation Centre.

title: Veteran programmer finishes retro game sequel and publishes it after 37 years — Colin Porch started Head Over Heels home computer title in 1989, but it was shelved due to console pivot
summary: Hit 1980s isometric puzzler adventure game gets a sequel 37 years later. The original programmer is now over 80.

title: Prototype of the ‘world’s first fluid circuit board’ can be physically rewired in less than a minute, startup claims — could make hardware iteration 1,000 times faster than traditional PCB
summary: A deep tech startup has come out of stealth brandishing a prototype of what it claims to be 'the world’s first fluid circuit board.'

title: The Sunderland footballer setting record straight after 46 years
